items.title: Zambry wants student startups to go global under new higher education entrepreneurship plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) launched the Higher Education Institution Entrepreneurship Action Plan (PTK-IPT) 2026-2030 yesterday. The plan aims to build a sustainable, inclusive, and high-impact entrepreneurship ecosystem across universities to produce globally competitive, holistic entrepreneurs. Minister Datuk Seri Dr. Zambry Abd Kadir said the plan aligns with the Malaysia Higher Education Plan 2026-2035, which prioritises educational flexibility, tech mastery, lifelong learning, innovation and future talent development.

items.title: Govt rolls out two-day Rukun Negara camps for youths aged 18 to 30 starting this month
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 16 — The Rukun Negara Youth Tour 2026 programme will implemented from this month to October, involving 15 selected higher learning institutions nationwide in an effort to strengthen unity, patriotism and self-confidence of Malaysian youth. National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang said the programme was implemented through an interactive and experience-based approach to strengthen appreciation of the values of the Rukun Negara in daily life. Aaron also included an infographic on the programme organised by the Ministry of National Unity through the Department of National Unity and Integration, which targets the participation of youth, aged 18 to 30.

items.title: Over 200 food courts, hawker centres nationwide to be upgraded by December
items.summary: Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the projects under his ministry involved costs ranging between RM300,000 and RM500,000. "The Housing and Local Government Ministry approved an allocation of RM60 million to upgrade more than 200 food courts and hawker centres. "Over the past three years, the ministry has also completed 15,082 infrastructure projects nationwide.

items.title: ‘Profits’ looked real until withdrawals failed: Two in Penang lose RM2.7m to China-linked investment schemes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 16 — Two senior citizens including a former company director lost a total of almost RM2.7 million after being duped by online investment syndicates that promised lucrative returns through separate investment platforms, recently. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the first case involved a 62-year-old man who worked as a machinery attendant and suffered a loss of RM1.294 million after being scammed in an online investment scheme known as “Global Finance Trends XA”. In a separate case, he said a former female company director, 72, also lost RM1.403 million after being scammed by an online investment syndicate using the name “Shanghai Futures Exchange”.

items.title: Pangkor migrant boat sinking: Authorities now believe 39 were onboard, raising fears more may still be missing
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — Four more bodies were found today from the incident involving a boat carrying Indonesian migrants that capsized in the waters off Pulau Pangkor, bringing the death toll to 16 so far. Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) director Maritime Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob said three of the victims found were men and were discovered about 2.5 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Buloh, while another body, whose identity has yet to be confirmed, was found about 21.4 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Sangga Kechil. “Meanwhile, the other three victims were found by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) at about 5.45 pm,” he said when contacted by Bernama yesterday.

items.title: Starmer expected to approve US$24 billion increase in UK defence spending, The Times reports
items.summary: BRITISH Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to approve an increase of £18 billion (US$24 billion) in defence spending as he faces a battle for political survival, The Times reported on Friday. The government is still finalising a defence spending plan and will publish it as soon as possible, a spokesperson told Reuters. The British government's defence investment plan will aim to deliver equipment and technology to frontline forces quickly while supporting economic growth, the spokesperson said.

items.title: RM300,000 homes pile up unsold as Malaysia faces "affordable housing paradox"
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's "affordable housing" segment is facing a sharp contradiction, according to data from the National Property Information Centre (NAPIC), highlighting a growing disconnect in the property market. Despite sustained demand for homes priced below RM300,000, tens of thousands of completed units remain unsold, exposing what experts are calling a structural affordability and demand mismatch in the country's property market. Housing experts said the numbers point to a deeper issue: affordability is no longer defined by price tags alone but by whether households can realistically sustain total ownership costs in today's economic environment.

items.title: Malaysia's economy seen resilient in 2026, growth orojected at 4–5pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy is expected to remain resilient in 2026, with growth projected to be sustained at between four and five per cent despite ongoing global uncertainties. Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said the Malaysian economy recorded a strong growth of 5.4 per cent in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, driven mainly by domestic demand. Year-on-year (YoY), public consumption rose 4.1 per cent while public investment increased 5.3 per cent.

items.title: Factbox: Who is Raul Castro, the Cuban leader facing a US indictment
items.summary: CUBA: Former Cuban president Raul Castro , the 94-year-old brother of revolutionary leader Fidel Castro, could be indicted by the United States in connection with Cuba's deadly 1996 shootdown of planes operated by the humanitarian group Brothers to the Rescue. Castro served as his brother's defence minister for decades, building a powerful base within the military and Cuban state. [[nid:1440664]] A lasting power broker Castro served as president until 2018.

items.title: US plans to unveil criminal charges against Raul Castro next Wednesday
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration plans to announce criminal charges against former Cuban president Raul Castro next Wednesday, according to a US Justice Department official, in a move that would escalate the pressure campaign against the island's communist government. The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity, said federal prosecutors expect to unseal an indictment against Castro, 94, in Miami on May 20, based on a 1996 incident in which Cuban jets shot down planes operated by a group of Cuban exiles. Castro is the brother of the late Fidel Castro, the revolutionary and longtime foil of the United States who led the island's communist government for decades.

items.title: Authorities, developers must share blame for shrinking KL flood retention ponds [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The issue surrounding Kuala Lumpur's flood retention ponds losing capacity following the transfer of land to developers should be viewed from both legal and governance perspectives, and accountability should not rest solely on developers, analysts said. Yeoh had said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) would not approve any planning permission for development applications on flood retention pond land unless all conditions set by the Drainage and Irrigation Department (DID) were fully complied with. Najib said that, however, this does not automatically exempt developers from responsibility.

items.title: Principal took SPM Math exam 20 times to support students [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: While most teachers cheer their students on from outside the examination hall, one dedicated educator sat the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination with them more than 20 times. Principal Zulkifli Md Pungot has retaken the examination as a repeat candidate for Mathematics since 2011 to better support his students. It's more of an exercise to understand students' experience, pressure, syllabus changes and answering techniques.

items.title: Exocet, Atmaca among alternatives for LCS as Norway revokes missile export licence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: With Norway having revoked the licences for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system to be exported to Malaysia, a new question arises: What happens to the Royal Malaysian Navy's Littoral Combat Ships (LCS) without the weapon system intended to be the primary strike capability? The Defence Ministry had said it had begun exploring alternative surface-to-surface missile systems to ensure both the LCS and Lekiu -class frigates have credible offensive power. Zaki said this would not be the navy's first platforms that were FFBNW.

items.title: Ipoh junction now rocking ‘light saber’ traffic lights, drivers say it’s way safer (VIDEO)
items.summary: IPOH, May 16 — A striking new “light saber” traffic light system has appeared near Enclave Residences along Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah, following a spate of accidents allegedly linked to poor visibility at the junction. Mohammad Nizar said the illuminated traffic light system, the first of its kind to be installed in Ipoh and the Kinta district, complies with existing traffic light specifications and standards. “Taking into account complaints received regarding accidents allegedly caused by the lack of visibility of the existing traffic light system, the Public Works Department (JKR) introduced an innovative initiative by installing the ‘light saber’ style traffic light pole.

items.title: US moves to end job protections for hundreds of health department workers
items.summary: HHS employees at several agencies received the email, which said members of their teams would be affected by the reclassification, known as Schedule P/C and formerly as Schedule F. At HHS, the reclassification will initially apply to employees "on the order of hundreds, not thousands," the email said, with additional tranches to follow. An HHS official confirmed the email's authenticity and said it "reflects the finalisation of previously announced RIFs", referring to reductions in force or mass layoffs. The official said no new RIFs were planned.

items.title: Lebanon PM says country has had enough 'reckless' wars for foreign interests
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam on Friday said his country has had enough "reckless" wars for foreign interests, calling for Arab and international support in Beirut's negotiations with Israel. In an implicit rebuke to Hizbollah, which joined the Middle East war in support of Iran on March 2, Salam said the country had "enough of these reckless adventures serving foreign projects or interests, the latest being a war we did not choose but was forced upon us, which led to Israel occupying 68 towns and villages". Beirut last year committed to disarming Hizbollah after the group was weakened by its 2024 war with Israel.

items.title: Judge declares mistrial in Weinstein sex assault case
items.summary: NEW YORK: A US judge declared a mistrial in the trial of disgraced cinema mogul Harvey Weinstein after jurors again failed to reach a verdict on allegations he sexually assaulted actor Jessica Mann, prosecutors said. For nearly a decade, Jessica Mann has fought for justice," Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg said in a statement. 'Miracle' During the trial, Mann recalled how Weinstein showered her with compliments after the pair met at a party in early 2013, when Mann was an aspiring 27-year-old actor and Weinstein a Hollywood powerbroker.

items.title: Trump warns against Taiwan independence after China visit
items.summary: BEIJING: US President Donald Trump on Friday warned Taiwan against declaring formal independence after concluding his visit to China, whose leader Xi Jinping had pressed him not to support the self-ruling island. On a key issue for Xi, Trump made clear he opposed a declaration of independence by Taiwan and appeared to question why the United States would defend the island in case of attack. I want China to cool down," Trump said.

items.title: Israel, Lebanon say extending ceasefire despite new strikes
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend a ceasefire and hold expanded talks on a political settlement, the United States announced Friday, even as Israel carried out new strikes that it insists are not subject to the truce. Israel has been pounding Lebanon and invaded its south in response to fire from Hizbollah, the Iranian-backed Shia movement that is not part of the ceasefire diplomacy. Envoys from Israel and Lebanon's government, which has struggled to restrain Hizbollah, met for two days in Washington and said they would extend the ceasefire that was set to expire Sunday.

items.title: Boeing confirms China commitment to buy 200 aircraft
items.summary: NEW YORK: Aerospace giant Boeing on Friday confirmed that China had committed to purchasing 200 aircraft, as previously announced by US President Donald Trump during his visit to Beijing. "We had a very successful trip to China and accomplished our major goal of reopening the China market to orders for Boeing aircraft," the company, whose CEO Kelly Ortberg was part of the US delegation to China, said in a statement. "We now look forward to continually addressing China's aircraft demand," it said.

items.title: BRICS meeting ends without joint statement
items.summary: HYDERABAD: The two-day meeting of BRICS foreign ministers ended in New Delhi on Friday without a joint statement, highlighting sharp differences among members over the conflict in the Middle East. Instead, India issued a "chair's statement and outcome document" on the deliberations, reflecting a lack of consensus on the crisis, which has fuelled global economic and security concerns. "BRICS members expressed their respective national positions and shared a range of perspectives," the statement released by India noted.

items.title: Palestinian teenager killed by Israeli fire as settlers torch mosque in West Bank
items.summary: RAMALLAH: A Palestinian teenager was killed by Israeli army fire on Friday, while Israeli settlers torched a mosque and two vehicles in the West Bank, according to Palestinian sources. Palestinian security sources told Xinhua that Fahd Oweis, 16, was shot dead by Israeli forces in the town of al-Lubban al-Sharqiya, south of the northern West Bank city of Nablus. The sources added that Israeli forces withheld his body.

items.title: Iran: Strait of Hormuz is closed 'only to our enemies'
items.summary: TEHERAN/NEW DELHI: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open to shipping from all friendly countries, the German Press Agency (dpa) reported. Iran's chief diplomat also surprisingly held out the prospect of lifting restrictions imposed by the Iranian navy. According to Iran, the strait has also since been mined.

items.title: Husband clings to memories of wife killed 50 days after wedding
items.summary: Mohamad Khairil Akmal Md Rahim said the death of his wife, Siti Nurlieyana Azieka Rosli, 23, on Feb 10 remains deeply painful. Khairil said the road was dark at the time, although weather conditions were clear. He said his wife was trapped in the front passenger seat and suffered severe injuries.

items.title: Teen among 23 charged with organised crime in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING: Twenty-three men, including a teenager, believed to be members of an organised crime group involved in various violent criminal activities in Sarawak, were charged in three Sessions Courts here today. They are accused of being members of an organised crime group known as Geng 24 Hung-Hung between December 2021 and April last year. No plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court, consistent with offences under the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 (Sosma).

items.title: GOF detains 360 illegal immigrants in Kelantan since January
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade detained 360 illegal immigrants through Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan (OTWK) from Jan 1 to May 15 this year. GOF Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said Myanmar nationals accounted for the most arrests at 264, followed by 40 Thai nationals, 30 Bangladeshis, and nine Indonesians. "Identified hotspots include illegal jetties in Rantau Panjang, Pengkalan Kubor, Jeram Perdah, and Chabang Empat, frequently used as entry points by illegal immigrants.

items.title: Razali Idris remains Kijal assemblyman, no seat vacancy - Speaker
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: Datuk Razali Idris' position as the Kijal state assemblyman is secure and there will be no automatic vacancy, said Terengganu State Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Nor Hamzah. He said the issue of the Kijal state seat position arose following the Sessions Court's decision to fine Razali RM2,000 after he was found guilty of making inflammatory statements during his speech at the Kemaman By-Election Machinery Launching Ceremony, three years ago. – Bernama TAGS: ADUN, seat, Kijal, by-election, vacancy, court, fine © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Hot days coming as El Niño expected in June, lasting to 2027
items.summary: Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT) meteorology researcher Dr Chung Jing Xiang said the El Niño–Southern Oscillation (ENSO) climate system is currently in a weak La Niña phase but is gradually shifting towards neutral conditions before possibly turning into El Niño. Its three phases — La Niña, ENSO-neutral and El Niño — play a major role in shaping weather patterns globally, including in Southeast Asia. "At present, the southwest monsoon has yet to coincide with El Niño as ENSO remains in a weak La Niña to ENSO-neutral phase.

items.title: Retiree, machinery caretaker lose RM2.7mil in separate online scams
items.summary: PENANG: A retired company director and a machinery caretaker lost a combined RM2.697 million after falling victim to separate online investment scams promising lucrative returns. He said in the first case, a 72-year-old former company director in Penang lost RM1.403 million after being duped by a syndicate using the name "Shanghai Futures Exchange". He said the suspect later introduced the victim to an online investment scheme known as the "Shanghai Futures Exchange", which promised high returns within a short period.

items.title: Man held over illegal sale of wildlife parts in Kota Kinabalu
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A local man was arrested for allegedly selling wildlife parts believed to be from protected species during Op Antique at a business premises here on Wednesday. In a statement today, the Sabah Wildlife Department said the operation, conducted jointly with the police, led to the seizure of various wildlife parts believed to be from protected species, including 16 canine teeth suspected to be from wild boars, nine teeth believed to be from tigers, and 10 teeth suspected to be from bears. The integrated operation also involved Bukit Aman's Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Investigation Intelligence (WCB/PSK).

items.title: Ministry gathering proposals to implement POLYCC restructuring, says Zambry
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Higher Education Ministry is currently gathering proposals and findings to implement the restructuring of polytechnics and community colleges (POLYCC), in an effort to strengthen and improve these institutions. Its minister, Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir, said that the MOHE is currently reviewing several key aspects that require improvement, including salary issues, career pathways, professional development, and the positions of officers in the institutions. Kembara Karisma Pemimpin Muda Madani 2026, organised by the Higher Education Leadership Academy (AKEPT), is a strategic platform to strengthen the development of young leadership talents among lecturers and administrators of higher education institutions.

items.title: European airlines downplay fears of summer shortage
items.summary: EUROPEAN airlines, airports and tour operators are striking a bullish tone on jet fuel supply despite one of the worst crises in decades, which has driven prices to double from pre-Iran war levels as the conflict snarls oil flows via the Strait of Hormuz. The Gulf supplies around a quarter of Europe's jet fuel. But Lufthansa CEO Carsten Spohr said the company's fuel supplies were secured at least to early summer.

items.title: Seditious statement: Kijal assemblyman withdraws appeal against conviction, RM2,000 fine
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kijal assemblyman Datuk Razali Idris has withdrawn his appeal against the Sessions Court's decision to impose a fine of RM2,000 after he was found guilty of making inflammatory statements during a speech at the Kemaman By-Election Machinery Launching Ceremony three years ago. Lawyer Shahril Nizam Mohd Amin, who represented Razali, informed High Court Judge Datuk Azhar Abdul Hamid that his party had received further instructions from his client (Razali) to withdraw the notice of appeal filed on Jan 5. Azhar then withdrew the notice of appeal.

items.title: Malaysia is still the best place to call home
items.summary: Alas, when they do decide to come "home", they now must settle for the Malaysia My Second Home resident visa like other foreigners. It all makes me wonder if Malaysia has really changed, particularly Malaysians overseas who decide to return for good. But what likely changed is these Malaysians' perception of their home country, with the benefit of having experienced living overseas and, perhaps, viewing home now from fresher lenses.

items.title: Pangkor boat capsize: Four more bodies found, brings death toll to 16
items.summary: IPOH: Four more bodies were found today from the incident involving a boat carrying Indonesian migrants that capsized in the waters off Pulau Pangkor, bringing the death toll to 16 so far. Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) director Maritime Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob said three of the victims found were men and were discovered about 2.5 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Buloh, while another body, whose identity has yet to be confirmed, was found about 21.4 nautical miles southwest of Pulau Sangga Kechil. "Meanwhile, the other three victims were found by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) at about 5.45pm," he said when contacted by Bernama today.

items.title: Applications open June 30 as Putrajaya gives non‑mainstream students direct shot at public universities
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — Applications for students from outside the national education system to pursue studies at public institutions of higher learning (IPTA) will open on June 30. Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said that for this year, applications will be submitted directly to the respective universities before the process is integrated into the UPUOnline system in 2027. “InsyaAllah, we will open applications directly through the universities offering these programmes beginning June 30 to enable applications to be submitted this year,” she said at a special press conference on admission pathways for students from outside the national education system to public universities here today.

items.title: Sabah’s Lahad Datu–Tawau road collapse strands vehicles, diversion works underway at Jalan Silam
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, May 15 — The main road linking Lahad Datu and Tawau was cut off this evening after a 50-metre stretch of Jalan Silam here collapsed in both directions. Lahad Datu District Officer Firuz Idzualdeen @ Benetty Mohd Dzul said the road was temporarily impassable and there is currently no alternative route to either Tawau or Lahad Datu. — Bernama

items.title: Fadhlina: Teacher welfare matters, spouse‑distance transfers now handled quicker nationwide
items.summary: IPOH, May 15 — Transfer applications involving teachers seeking placement closer to their spouses remain among the key priorities in safeguarding teachers’ welfare, said Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek. She said the ministry has taken several steps to improve the handling of teacher welfare cases, including reducing the waiting period for transfer applications. “For cases involving long-distance spouses, the Ministry of Education gives priority.

items.title: Kedah police probe Bedong school fight involving female students, clarify video misled viewers
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, May 15 — Police have identified between six and 10 female students believed to be involved in a fight in front of a secondary school in Bedong, which went viral on social media since yesterday. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the incident involved secondary school students from Form One to Form Four, but initial investigations found that only two or three students were directly involved in the fight. “Our review of the viral video found that the actions of several students attempting to break up the fight created the impression that everyone at the scene was involved in the incident,” he told reporters here, today.

items.title: Fourth Malaysian‑linked ship transits Strait of Hormuz with Iranian clearance, shipping data shows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — A support vessel owned by Malaysian firm Vantris Energy, ‌formerly known as Sapura Energy, passed through the Strait of Hormuz, LSEG shipping data showed today, becoming the fourth ship linked to the country to transit ‌the waterway since the outbreak of the US-Iran war. Malaysia’s ‌Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said in March Iran would allow Malaysian ⁠vessels to pass through the strait ⁠after holding talks with Iranian officials. Authorities have ⁠said the vessels Malaysia had ⁠asked for clearance include ⁠those owned by Vantris, shipping giant MISC Berhad and state energy firm Petronas.

items.title: Education Ministry fills 50 school leadership posts, says vacancies will be addressed promptly
items.summary: Its Minister, Fadhlina Sidek, said the ministry views the issue seriously, stressing that schools cannot function effectively without strong leadership. She said strong leadership is crucial in implementing school reforms, ensuring safety, and managing daily school operations. She said the appointments involved schools in every state and took effect immediately.

items.title: MACC clears Krishnan Tan of wrongdoing, IJM chairman says he wants to move on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Tan Sri Krishnan Tan today confirmed that the Malaysian Anti‑Corruption Commission (MACC) has formally cleared him of wrongdoing, following the conclusion of investigations into the sale of IJM Corporation Berhad shares to Sunway Berhad. In a statement today, the IJM Corporation Berhad chairman said he welcomed the decision, adding that he had “steadfastly maintained” his innocence. Previously, IJM Corporation Berhad had clarified that it is not being investigated as a corporate entity, and that MACC’s probe was confined to several individuals linked to the group.

items.title: Homeowner, swimming club in Ipoh settle pickleball noise row with indoor facility plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — A homeowner and the Ipoh Swimming Club have reached a settlement in a lawsuit over noise from an outdoor pickleball court. The Ipoh High Court recorded a consent judgment before judge Moses Susayan, with the club agreeing that no open‑air pickleball games would be allowed in the area adjoining the house, as reported by Free Malaysia Today. Cheang had filed the suit earlier this year, claiming the club built a pickleball court next to his home on Lorong Gopeng at Taman Golf.

items.title: Ex-polytechnic student walks free after judge bins 3.4kg drug trafficking charge, says key witness not credible
items.summary: Judge Datuk Azhar Abdul Hamid made the ruling after finding that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against Harith Izzuddin Mohd Hanizam, 23, at the end of its case. In delivering his decision, Azhar said the court found that the prosecution had relied on the testimony of the fourth prosecution witness (SP4), the complainant who is also a police officer, to prove the element of possession. “Therefore, I ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case against the accused.

items.title: From service to second career: WBL programme helps military veterans build future prospects
items.summary: MASJID TANAH, May 15 — The Defence Ministry is expanding its Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme to help military personnel secure better-paying and more stable jobs after retirement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said many Armed Forces personnel enter service with only the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification and, without intervention, retire with the same academic credentials despite accumulating decades of skills and experience during their service. “Most military personnel join the Armed Forces with only an SPM qualification.

items.title: Home minister says drug problem in Langkawi real, but not like Pablo Escobar’s cartel
items.summary: KULIM, May 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has denied a news report that likened drug trafficking activities in Langkawi to the operations of an international cartel. According to him, the magnitude of drug trafficking on the resort island cannot be likened to the drug cartel led by drug trafficker Pablo Escobar who controls the entire drug trafficking complex. Commenting on the proposal of Kedah Menteri Besar, Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor for the police to shoot drug dealer, Saifuddin Nasution said the drug issue needs to be addressed rationally.

items.title: DBKL gets RM1.5m for 100 bus stops after roadside wait complaints, transport minister expects faster rollout than Penang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Ministry of Transport (MOT) approved an additional RM1.5 million allocation to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to construct and upgrade bus stops across the federal capital. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the move follows complaints from public transport users about the lack of proper bus facilities at several locations in Kuala Lumpur. Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah, and Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: MACC says no corruption found in KL flood retention pond land conversion probe after Hannah Yeoh query
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no elements of corruption in its investigation into the conversion of land status involving flood retention ponds in the capital, according to a report by Berita Harian . The probe centred on allegations of abuse of power and irregularities involving approvals for development projects near flood retention ponds in Kuala Lumpur, including in Taman Wahyu. “Among the allegations investigated was the conversion of the status of several areas near flood retention ponds in the capital, including in Taman Wahyu, for development purposes,” he reportedly said today.

items.title: Navy recovers 11th body from capsized Indonesian boat off Pulau Pangkor, three still missing
items.summary: IPOH, May 15 — Another body of a victim from a boat carrying Indonesian migrants that capsized off the waters of Pulau Pangkor was found this morning, bringing the number of recovered bodies to 11 so far. The Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said the body of a male victim was discovered by the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) at 9.27 am, located 5.6 nautical miles from the incident site. The SAR operation involved seven assets, namely the Maritime Vessel KM Malawali (MMEA), Benteng 1203 (MMEA), KD Sri Indera Sakti (RMN), FCB 1001 (RMN), FCB 1030, PSC 16 (Marine Police) and PSC 24 (Marine Police), focusing on locating three more missing victims.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues severe weather warning for KL, Selangor and parts of Johor, Sabah till 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning affecting Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and nine states until 6pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Perak are Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kinta, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. The warning also covers parts of Terengganu, namely Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman, as well as Pahang involving Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Bera and Rompin, in addition to Selangor.

items.title: Johor royal family attends Berandam Surai ceremony for youngest princess
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 15 — A Berandam Surai ceremony for the youngest daughter of Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, and his consort, Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Bustamam, was held at Istana Bukit Serene here today. Also present was the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail, and the other royal children. Tunku Khadeeja Khayra Iskandariah Tunku Ismail was born on April 5.

items.title: Must pass SPM BM and History first: Anwar confirms UEC students can enter public universities
items.summary: He said all students, regardless of their schooling stream, must meet the basic requirement of passing Bahasa Melayu and History subjects at the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) level. “The Cabinet decision on SMPC/UEC comes with the condition that students pass Bahasa Melayu and History subjects in SPM. “This does not apply only to SMPC/UEC students, but also to students from Arabic schools, secondary religious schools, tahfiz institutions and others.

items.title: College student, 24, faces murder charge over couple’s death in Jalan Ampang crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — A college student was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a married couple following a fatal crash along Jalan Ampang earlier this month. Danish Raman Kamar Kumaresan, 24, nodded in understanding after the charges were read before Magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri. The accused is alleged to have caused the deaths of A. Doraisingam, 61, and A. Menagah, 56, in a crash along Jalan Ampang towards Ampang Point from KLCC at 5.30 am on May 2.

items.title: On national Youth Day, Anwar announces RM5m allocation for Youth Council
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced a RM5 million allocation for the Malaysian Youth Council (MBM) to strengthen the umbrella body’s role in advancing youth development in the country. He said the additional RM1 million allocation compared with last year was recognition of MBM’s role as the main driver of the national youth agenda under the Ministry of Youth and Sports (KBS), while reflecting the government’s continued commitment to supporting youth development programmes. Also present were Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

items.title: New MACC chief Halim Aman vows stronger cooperation with AGC on high-profile cases
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will continue strengthening its strategic cooperation with the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC), particularly in handling high-profile, large-scale and public interest cases. Newly-appointed MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said such cases would continue to be referred to the AGC as the authority responsible for determining legal action on investigations conducted by the anti-graft agency. Meanwhile, Mohd Dusuki expressed appreciation for Abd Halim’s decision to make the AGC his first courtesy visit as the new MACC chief commissioner.

items.title: Jho Low pardon bid: Anwar says ‘we are not going that route’, leaves US decision to Washington
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia will not consider any pardon application involving fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low. Asked whether the government would formally oppose any move by the United States (US) regarding the matter, Anwar said: “As far as we are concerned, we are not going that route...but of course, what the US decides is their own business.” The Prime Minister also said that he was unaware of any pardon application submitted by Jho Low. According to the report, approval of the application would result in the US criminal charges against Jho Low being dropped.

items.title: Anthony Loke labels backlash against UEC public university access an attempt to politicise education
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today slammed criticism over the move to open public universities to graduates of Chinese Independent High Schools (SMPC) and Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) holders, describing it as an attempt to politicise education. Yesterday, the Higher Education Ministry (KPT) announced that students from outside the national education system, including UEC holders, would be allowed to enrol in public universities, marking a watershed decision as the UEC system has long been a flashpoint in race relations. He claimed that combining tahfiz graduates and UEC holders under the same announcement was a subtle attempt to legitimise the admission of UEC students into public universities.

items.title: Tiger teeth, bear fangs among RM5.32m wildlife parts seized in Kota Kinabalu raid
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 15 — Sabah wildlife authorities believe they have uncovered an illegal wildlife parts trading operation in the heart of the city following a raid on an antique shop along Jalan Gaya here two days ago. In a statement today, Sabah Wildlife Department director Soffian Abu Bakar said the raid uncovered several wildlife parts from protected species, including 16 tusks believed to be from wild boars, nine teeth believed to be from tigers, 10 teeth suspected to be from bears, various edible bird’s nest products of different weights, as well as 13 stones believed to be from porcupines. The case is being investigated under Sections 41(1) and 41(2) of the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for allegedly possessing, storing and displaying wildlife parts without valid permits.

items.title: RM57m in luxury houses, handbags, watches, cars seized as police crack international scam ring based in Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Police have arrested 187 suspects from nine countries in a major crackdown on international online scam syndicates operating out of the Klang Valley. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Khalid Ismail said the operation, dubbed Op Teguh 2.0, involved 46 raids on residential premises believed to be used as bases for cross-border scam activities. Khalid said the groups used several scam tactics, including fake investment platforms targeting victims in Hong Kong and China, impersonation scams involving fake Japanese police officers on Telegram, romance scams aimed at victims in Korea and Japan, and illegal online gambling operations.

items.title: Drugs, phone, weapon found inside Ipoh police lock-up, Bukit Aman orders full investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail has ordered a full investigation after drugs and other prohibited items were found inside a police lock-up in Ipoh, Perak. I have instructed district police chiefs to inspect lock-ups from time to time. “All state police chiefs have the authority to conduct inspections, and district police chiefs also carry out the same duties.
